title: Bot Manager & AI Scraping
subtitle: Defending content from AI crawlers and training data harvesters

steps.label: The Analogy
steps.body: AI scraper bots are like tourists who photograph every painting in a museum, then sell prints without permission. Bot Manager is the museum security team — it distinguishes genuine visitors from commercial photographers and enforces the rules.

steps.label: How It Works
steps.body:
Akamai Bot Manager uses ML-based behavioral analysis to classify bots:

- **Known bots**: Googlebot, Bingbot — allow or throttle per policy
- **AI training crawlers**: GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Common Crawl — block or serve alternate content
- **Aggressive scrapers**: Headless browsers, rotating proxies — fingerprint and challenge

New AI-specific features:
- AI crawler identification by user-agent and behavioral fingerprint
- robots.txt enforcement at the edge (many AI crawlers ignore it)
- Selective content serving: show crawlers a stripped version, real users get the full site

steps.label: Real World
steps.body: Publishers and media companies face a dilemma: block AI crawlers entirely (lose AI search visibility) or allow everything (donate content for free). Bot Manager enables a middle path: allow indexing by approved crawlers, block training-data harvesters, and rate-limit the rest. Revenue-critical for any content business.
"Bot Manager = ML-powered bot classification. In the AI era, it's the gatekeeper deciding which crawlers see your content and on what terms."

quiz.question: A publisher wants to appear in AI-powered search results but doesn't want their content used for model training. What's the Bot Manager strategy?

options.explain: Blocking everything kills search visibility too.

options.text: Allow approved search crawlers (Googlebot), block training-data crawlers (GPTBot, Common Crawl), rate-limit unknowns
options.explain: Granular policy: search indexing yes, training scraping no, unknowns get challenged.

options.text: Add a robots.txt and hope for the best
options.explain: Many AI crawlers ignore robots.txt. Edge enforcement is more reliable.

options.text: Remove all content from the site

quiz.question: How does Bot Manager distinguish an AI training crawler from a legitimate search bot?

options.explain: IP is one signal, but bots rotate IPs. Behavioral analysis is the key.

options.text: ML-based behavioral fingerprinting: request patterns, user-agent, JavaScript execution, mouse movement
options.explain: Multi-signal ML classification combines user-agent, request cadence, JS capabilities, and behavioral patterns.
Referrer is easily spoofed and unreliable for classification.
